content:
[S1]: Antibiotics are medicines that help us fight germs that make us sick.  
[READER]: Oh, I know about medicine! So antibiotics are special medicine for germs. I wonder how germs can fight back?  

[S2]: But sometimes, germs learn how to protect themselves from antibiotics, and this is called antibiotic resistance.  
[READER]: Huh, germs can learn? That sounds strange but interesting. How do they do that?  

[S3]: Germs can change little parts of themselves so the medicine doesn't work anymore.  
[READER]: Wow, germs are like tiny superheroes that can change! But why do they change?  

[S4]: This happens a lot when people don't finish their medicine or use antibiotics when they don't need them.  
[READER]: Oh, so people can make germs stronger by not using medicine properly? That sounds important!  

[S5]: Also, when animals on farms get antibiotics too much, germs there can become resistant and spread to people.  
[READER]: I didn’t know animals could be part of this! That’s a bit scary. What can we do to stop it?  

[S6]: To help stop antibiotic resistance, we should only use antibiotics when a doctor says we need them.  
[READER]: That makes sense! So doctors know when to use antibiotics. What else should I remember?  

[S7]: Washing our hands and staying healthy can also keep germs away so we don’t need antibiotics as much.  
[READER]: Oh, so simple things like washing hands can help! I can do that every day.  

[S8]: If we all do these things, antibiotics will keep working to help us stay healthy for a long time.  
[READER]: Yay! I want antibiotics to keep working so I don’t get sick. I’ll tell my family too!
